name: Las Palmas, Gran Canaria
summary: Las Palmas is the biggest city on Gran Canaria, a sunny volcanic island in the Atlantic Ocean off the coast of Africa. It hugs a long golden beach called Las Canteras and has an old town where Christopher Columbus once stopped on his way to America.
funFacts: Christopher Columbus stopped on Gran Canaria in 1492 to fix a broken ship rudder, and a museum called Casa de Colon now tells the story.
Gran Canaria is nicknamed a 'continent in miniature' because in one short drive you can pass beaches, pine forests, deserts and tall volcanic mountains.
The Maspalomas Dunes in the south look like a tiny Sahara desert that meets the sea, with golden sand stretching for kilometres beside the Atlantic.
On nearby La Gomera island people talk by whistling a special language called Silbo Gomero, loud enough to send messages right across deep valleys.
The Gran Canaria giant lizard lives only on this island and can grow up to 80 centimetres long, including its very long whippy tail.
thingsToDo: Splash and build sandcastles at Playa de Las Canteras, a long golden beach with a natural reef that calms the waves.
Visit Casa de Colon in the old town to see ship models and learn how Columbus prepared for his big 1492 voyage.
Wander the cobbled lanes of Vegueta to Santa Ana cathedral and climb its tower for a view across orange-tiled rooftops.
Meet sharks, jellyfish and tropical fish at Poema del Mar aquarium near the cruise port, with a giant curved viewing window.
Drive up into the mountains to see Roque Nublo, a huge rock pillar standing tall in the middle of the island.
Try Canarian papas arrugadas, salty wrinkly potatoes dunked in spicy green or red mojo sauce, at a friendly cafe.

wildlife.name: Atlantic Canary
wildlife.description: Small yellow-green wild songbird that gave the Canary Islands their name with its bright cheerful chirping.

wildlife.name: Gran Canaria Giant Lizard
wildlife.description: Long scaly reptile found only on Gran Canaria, basking on warm volcanic rocks in the sunshine.

wildlife.name: Loggerhead Sea Turtle
wildlife.description: Large brown sea turtle that swims through Canary Islands waters and sometimes nests on the beaches.

wildlife.name: Short-finned Pilot Whale
wildlife.description: Black dolphin-like whale that lives in family groups in the deep seas around the Canary Islands.

localWord.meaning: the Canarian word for 'bus' instead of the usual Spanish 'autobus'

images.alt: A long sandy beach in Las Palmas with calm blue water and people walking along the shore.
Colourful historic streets and buildings in the old Vegueta district of Las Palmas.
The grand stone cathedral of Santa Ana rising above the plaza in old Las Palmas.
The Casa de Colon museum in Las Palmas, a historic building with carved stone doorways.
Golden sand dunes rolling like a tiny desert at Maspalomas on Gran Canaria.
The towering Roque Nublo rock standing tall above the green mountains of Gran Canaria.
A small yellow-green wild Atlantic canary perched on a branch.
A large Gran Canaria giant lizard sunning itself on a rock.
A loggerhead sea turtle swimming through clear blue ocean water.
Common dolphins leaping above the waves of the Atlantic Ocean.
